stories.title: Memorial Tournament Preview — Scheffler Chases Historic Three-Peat at Muirfield Village

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
THREE-PEAT watch begins Thursday.

Scottie Scheffler (+300) at the Memorial Tournament — defending champion 2024 AND 2025.

Only Tiger Woods ever won this event 3 straight times (1999-2001).

Muirfield Village. June 4-7. $20M purse. #MemorialTournament #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
McIlroy at the Memorial: back-to-back Masters champion who has NEVER won at Muirfield Village.

He's +900 this week. Can the man who just made Augusta history finally break through in Dublin, Ohio?

#MemorialTournament #RoryMcIlroy

facebook.long_form:
One of the most compelling stories in professional golf begins Thursday at Muirfield Village.

Scottie Scheffler arrives at the Memorial Tournament as the two-time defending champion. He won this event in 2024 and again in 2025, becoming the first player since Tiger Woods (1999-2001) to claim back-to-back Memorial titles. Now he goes for three in a row — a feat only Woods has accomplished on Jack Nicklaus' course.

Scheffler is the World No. 1 and the odds-on favorite at +300 heading into Thursday's first round. He has one win in 2026 (at The American Express) and enters Muirfield Village seeking to jumpstart a season that has produced several runner-up finishes without a second win. The Memorial would be a statement.

Standing between Scheffler and history is a field that includes Rory McIlroy (+900), the back-to-back Masters champion who has never won at Muirfield Village. McIlroy is making only his eighth start of the 2026 season. He won Augusta in April with one of the great clutch performances in recent major history — a bogey on 18 to hold off Scheffler by one shot. That battle continues in Dublin, Ohio.

The full field includes Xander Schauffele (+1600), Cameron Young (+1600), and Ludvig Åberg (+1750). The $20 million purse — $4 million to the winner — is among the largest in golf.

Muirfield Village Golf Club: Par 72, 7,553 yards, one of the most demanding tests on the PGA Tour. First round tees off Thursday, June 4.
facebook.image_caption:
The Memorial Tournament is HERE. 🏆

Scottie Scheffler goes for a three-peat. Rory McIlroy comes in as back-to-back Masters champ. $20 million on the line.

Muirfield Village. June 4-7. #MemorialTournament #PGATour

article.headline: Memorial Tournament 2026 Preview: Scheffler Chases a Three-Peat Only Tiger Woods Has Accomplished

research.facts:
Memorial Tournament Preview
**Source:** Golf Channel, CBS Sports, Golf.com, The Memorial Tournament official site, TSN
**Key Facts:**
- Event: Memorial Tournament presented by Workday
- Dates: June 4-7, 2026
- Venue: Muirfield Village Golf Club, Dublin, OH
- Course: Par 72, 7,553 yards (designed by Jack Nicklaus, opened 1974)
- Purse: $20 million ($4M winner's share) — signature event
- Field: 72 players
- Scheffler: World No. 1, two-time defending champion (2024, 2025)
- Scheffler: Odds +300, one win in 2026 (The American Express), searching for 2nd win
- Three-peat would equal Tiger Woods (1999-2001); last three-peat at same event: Steve Stricker at John Deere Classic
- McIlroy: Back-to-back Masters champion (2025 and 2026); +900 odds; never won at Muirfield Village; eighth start of 2026 season
- McIlroy won 2026 Masters by 1 over Scheffler (bogey on 18th hole), was 6 shots ahead at 36 holes
- Other contenders: Xander Schauffele +1600; Cameron Young +1600; Ludvig Åberg +1750
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple verified sources)

stories.title: US Women's Open Preview — Korda Chases the Major That Has Eluded Her at Historic Riviera

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The US Women's Open begins Thursday at Riviera — the first women's major ever held there.

Korda (+350): 3 wins, 3 T2s in 7 starts this year. Never won the USWO.

This is the one. #USWomensOpen #NellyKorda #LPGA

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda has THREE career majors. But NOT the US Women's Open.

She finished T2 at last year's edition. She's the +350 favorite this week at Riviera.

7 starts in 2026. 3 wins. 3 T2s. The USWO is the missing piece.

#USWomensOpen #LPGA

facebook.long_form:
The 81st US Women's Open begins Thursday at Riviera Country Club in Pacific Palisades, California — and history will be made before a single shot is struck.

For the first time ever, a USGA women's major championship is being played at Riviera Country Club. It is a venue synonymous with PGA Tour history, the Los Angeles Open, and some of the most celebrated rounds in professional men's golf. Now the world's best women's players will compete there for a major title.

Nelly Korda enters as the clear favorite at +350. Her 2026 season is genuinely historic in its consistency: three wins — including the Chevron Championship major — and three runner-up finishes in just seven starts. She is the World No. 1. She is the most dominant player in women's golf.

And she has never won the US Women's Open. She finished second in 2025. That near-miss, on the biggest stage in her sport, makes this week especially charged.

Jeeno Thitikul is next at +900. The 23-year-old Thai star has two wins in 2026 and has been searching for a breakthrough major title throughout her career. Riviera is a ballstriker's course — par 71, 6,699 yards — and the field that arrives there this week is the best in women's golf.

First round: Thursday, June 4.
facebook.image_caption:
The US Women's Open is HERE — and it's making history at Riviera Country Club. ⛳

Korda (+350). Thitikul (+900). The world's best at one of the world's most iconic venues.

June 4-7, Pacific Palisades. #USWomensOpen #LPGA

article.headline: US Women's Open 2026 Preview: Korda Chases History at Riviera's First Women's Major

stories.title: LIV Golf Andalucia — Rahm and DeChambeau at Valderrama Before the US Open

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf heads to Valderrama this week — the last stop before the US Open.

Jon Rahm (16/5). Bryson DeChambeau (15/2). Niemann (8/1) chasing a record 9th LIV title.

And Phil Mickelson is out with a family health matter. #LIVGolf #Valderrama

facebook.long_form:
LIV Golf arrives at one of the most celebrated courses in European golf this week, and the timing couldn't be more meaningful.

Real Club Valderrama in Sotogrande, Spain — host of the 1997 Ryder Cup and widely called the "Augusta of Europe" — welcomes the LIV Golf field for the Andalucia event, June 4-7. It is the ninth event of the 2026 LIV season, and crucially, it is the last major preparation stop for LIV players ahead of the US Open.

Jon Rahm is the 16/5 favorite. The Spanish superstar is returning to his home country, captaining Legion XIII — the reigning LIV team champions. This is the course and the crowd that feels like home for Rahm, and he will be motivated accordingly.

Bryson DeChambeau is next at 15/2, with Joaquin Niemann at 8/1. Niemann arrives with a record eight LIV individual titles — the most in league history. A win this week would push that record to nine.

Sergio Garcia captains Fireballs GC on his home turf, alongside Spanish players David Puig and Josele Ballester. The home-nation angle runs deep at this event.

One notable absence: Phil Mickelson is not playing, with LIV citing an undisclosed family health matter as the reason.

$20 million individual purse. $10 million team purse. No cut. 57 players. Four days of championship golf at Valderrama.
facebook.image_caption:
LIV Golf goes to Valderrama this week — one of Europe's greatest golf courses. 🏌️

Rahm at home. Niemann chasing a record 9th title. DeChambeau in the hunt.

June 4-7, Spain. #LIVGolf

stories.title: McIlroy at the Memorial — Back-to-Back Masters Champion Seeks His First Muirfield Win

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy has won the Masters twice in a row. He has NEVER won the Memorial Tournament.

This week at Muirfield Village is only his 8th start of 2026.

Back-to-back Augusta champ vs. the course he can't crack. #MemorialTournament

facebook.long_form:
Rory McIlroy has won the Masters twice in a row. He became only the fourth player in Masters history to win consecutive green jackets. He held off Scottie Scheffler with a bogey on the 72nd hole in one of the most dramatic conclusions Augusta National has seen in years.

He has never won the Memorial Tournament.

That is the contradiction McIlroy carries to Muirfield Village this week. The Memorial is one of the most prestigious events on the PGA Tour — hosted by Jack Nicklaus, carrying the weight of the sport's greatest ambassadors, played on a course that genuinely rewards shotmaking and course management.

McIlroy is making only his eighth start of the 2026 PGA Tour season. He has been selective about his schedule since winning Augusta in April. He played the PGA Championship and finished five behind champion Aaron Rai, unable to add the second major of the year. Now he comes to Ohio, +900 to win, looking for a first Memorial title.

The McIlroy-Scheffler rivalry is one of the defining features of professional golf in 2026. They battled down the final holes at Augusta. They will be paired close to each other at Muirfield Village again. Whether the back-to-back Masters champion can finally break through in Dublin, Ohio is one of the story's most compelling threads heading into Thursday.
facebook.image_caption:
Back-to-back Masters champion. Four major wins. Never won the Memorial Tournament.

Rory McIlroy arrives at Muirfield Village this Thursday. Is this the week he fixes that? ⛳

#MemorialTournament #RoryMcIlroy

article.headline: McIlroy Brings Back-to-Back Masters Glory to the Memorial: Can He Finally Win at Muirfield Village?

- Had 6-shot lead at 36 holes, then blew lead before holding on
- Fourth player in history to win Masters in consecutive years
- McIlroy has 4 major titles (US Open 2011, Open 2014, PGA 2012, PGA 2014... wait, need to be careful here)
- McIlroy's Memorial odds: +900
- Has never won at Muirfield Village
- Only eighth start of 2026 season (limited schedule since Augusta win)
- Previously entered PGA Championship chasing Grand Slam — finished 5 back of Aaron Rai
- Confidence: HIGH

stories.title: Nelly Korda's 2026 Season — Three Wins, Three T2s, and the Major Still to Come

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda in 2026: 3 wins + 3 second-place finishes in just 7 starts.

The one thing missing? The US Women's Open.

Riviera starts Thursday. The most dominant player in women's golf enters as the favorite.

#LPGA #NellyKorda #USWomensOpen

facebook.long_form:
In seven starts on the LPGA Tour in 2026, Nelly Korda has finished first, first, first, second, second, first, and second.

That is a statistical record for consistency at the top of professional women's golf that has not been matched in this era. Three wins and three runner-up finishes means that in seven tournaments, Korda has stood in the top two on the leaderboard all but one time.

The three wins include the Chevron Championship, the first major of the LPGA season, which is now the third major championship of her career. She is the World No. 1 by a comfortable margin.

And she has never won the US Women's Open.

It is the one gap in a résumé that grows more impressive every week. She finished T2 at last year's edition — close enough to feel the sting, not close enough to hold the trophy. Now she arrives at Riviera Country Club as the +350 favorite to win the 81st edition of the major.

The venue is historic: the first time the US Women's Open has ever been held at Riviera. The course — par 71, 6,699 yards, a ballstriker's challenge — suits players who drive accurately and attack pins from the correct positions. That describes Korda precisely.

She is six days away from potentially completing the most dominant start to a season in women's golf history with a US Women's Open win. Riviera starts Thursday.
facebook.image_caption:
3 wins. 3 T2s. 7 starts. One major still missing.

Nelly Korda at the US Women's Open — Riviera Country Club, Thursday.

Can she finally add the USWO to her collection? ⛳ #NellyKorda #USWomensOpen

article.headline: Nelly Korda's 2026 Dominance: Three Wins, Three T2s, and One Major Still to Win
